report_name: Toy Giant Hasbro Investigating Cybersecurity Incident After Network Breach

identities.name: Hasbro, Inc.
identities.description: Global toy and entertainment company that detected unauthorized access to its corporate network on March 28, 2026, and subsequently took systems offline for containment.

indicators.name: Mimikatz Execution Detection
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Mimikatz Execution
id: 5e9a8f7c-1b2d-4c3e-8a9b-0f1e2d3c4b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the credential dumping tool Mimikatz, as suggested for hunting in the report.
references:
  - https://news.tradingview.com/en/hasbro-reports-network-breach-containment-measures-and-possible-operational-delays/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, but could be part of authorized red-teaming activities.
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mimikatz.exe'
    OriginalFileName: 'mimikatz.exe'
  condition: 1 of selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence or execution of the common credential dumping tool Mimikatz, as suggested in the article's detection guidance.

indicators.name: Active Directory Domain Admins Group Discovery
indicators.pattern:
title: Active Directory Domain Admins Group Discovery
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'net group' to enumerate members of the 'Domain Admins' group, a common reconnaissance technique.
references:
  - https://news.tradingview.com/en/hasbro-reports-network-breach-containment-measures-and-possible-operational-delays/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1069.002
  - attack.discovery
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'group'
      - 'Domain Admins'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects reconnaissance commands to map network permissions, specifically querying the 'Domain Admins' group, as suggested in the article's detection guidance.

indicators.name: System User Discovery via Whoami
indicators.pattern:
title: System User Discovery via Whoami
id: f1e2d3c4-b5a6-4f7e-8d9c-0b1a2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'whoami /all' to gather detailed information about the current user context, a common reconnaissance technique.
references:
  - https://news.tradingview.com/en/hasbro-reports-network-breach-containment-measures-and-possible-operational-delays/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1033
  - attack.discovery
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative or diagnostic activity.
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\whoami.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/all'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the 'whoami /all' command for system and user reconnaissance, as suggested in the article's detection guidance.

indicators.name: Potential RDP Lateral Movement
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential RDP Lateral Movement Between Workstations
id: cba98765-4321-4fed-bcba-9876543210fe
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections using the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) between non-server systems, which can indicate lateral movement.
references:
  - https://news.tradingview.com/en/hasbro-reports-network-breach-containment-measures-and-possible-operational-delays/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1021.001
  - attack.lateral_movement
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate remote administration between workstations.
  - Help desk support scenarios.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort: 3389
    Initiated: true
  filter_to_server:
    DestinationIp|startswith:
      - '10.'
      - '192.168.'
      - '172.16.'
      - '172.17.'
      - '172.18.'
      - '172.19.'
      - '172.20.'
      - '172.21.'
      - '172.22.'
      - '172.23.'
      - '172.24.'
      - '172.25.'
      - '172.26.'
      - '172.27.'
      - '172.28.'
      - '172.29.'
      - '172.30.'
      - '172.31.'
  condition: selection and not filter_to_server
indicators.description: Detects unusual lateral movement using RDP between workstations, which is not typical user behavior, as suggested in the article's detection guidance.
